title: Tasty Brazilian Shrimp Bao
description: A kid-friendly, Brazilian-inspired shrimp bao dish with peppery flavors and melted cheese. Perfect for a Buddhist temple-friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat a large skillet or griddle over medium-high heat.
Add the coconut oil and swirl to coat the pan.
Add the garlic powder and paprika, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the sliced red onion and jalapeño, and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until softened.
Add the jumbo shrimp and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until pink and cooked through.
Warm the corn tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the shrimp baos by placing a cooked shrimp onto a warmed tortilla, followed by a sprinkle of cilantro, a squeeze of lime juice, and a sprinkle of shredded cheese.
Fold the bottom half of the tortilla up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the bao into a neat cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients, and serve immediately.
Serve with additional lime wedges and cilantro, if desired.
